title: coreopsis aurea var. leptophylla
intro: Coreopsis aurea var. leptophylla, also known as the golden tickseed or small-flowered tickseed, is a perennial plant species belonging to the aster family that is native to the Great Plains of North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The medicinal properties of Coreopsis aurea var. leptophylla are said to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and supporting the immune system, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. Herbal preparations of Coreopsis aurea var. leptophylla, such as infusions and tinctures, can be made by drying and processing the plant's leaves, flowers, and roots, but caution should be exercised when using this plant for medicinal purposes due to potential interactions with other medications and allergies.
